verdict_reasoning: HSPA1A (HSP70) is canonically a cytosolic chaperone, but it is well-documented to translocate to the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane under stress and especially on tumor cells, where membrane-bound HSP70 is a recognized cancer-cell ecto-marker targeted by antibodies (e.g., cmHsp70.1) and NK-cell therapies. This cancer/stress-induced ecto-display qualifies as contextual surface accessibility.
